free_text: Measurement of the neutron.induced total cross
sections of natPb from 0.3 eV to 20 MeV on the Back-n
at CSNS
University of South China, Hengyang
Spallation Neutron Source Science Center,
Dongguan
The back-streaming white neutron
beam line (Back-n) at the China Spallation Neutron
Source (CSNS)
Neutrons were produced by 1.6 GeV proton-
induced spallation reactions on a tungsten target.
The incident proton pulse was in two bunches,
approximately 70 ns (FWHM) wide and 410 ns apart.
A high-purity (99.99%) cylindrical natural lead with
a thickness of 10.03 mm, diameter of 70 mm, mass of
436.8 g, density of 11.32 g/cm3.
The multi-cell fission chamber can be filled
with similar or different 8 pieces of samples, and
four 235 U and four 238 U high-purity samples were
used. The chamber was filled with a gas mixture of
90% Ar and 10% CF4 at 800 mbar.
Flight distance of 55 m. The cumulative
irradiation time for the sample was 142 h and 50 h
for the measurement without the sample.
uncertainty related to the statistical
uncertainty in the neutron counts and the
double-bunch unfolding process
uncertainty mainly comes from the
uncertainty of the atomic density of the sample and
the transmission
